commit.message:
Add exit confirmation dialog and deep link handling

Exit dialog:
- Pressing Back shows a full-screen dimmed overlay with
  'Keep Watching' and 'Exit' buttons
- Left/Right arrows move focus between buttons (default: Keep Watching)
- OK confirms the selection; Back dismisses (same as Keep Watching)
- Header stays visible while dialog is open; auto-hide resumes on dismiss
- Uses m.top.exitChannel = true for clean Roku channel exit

Deep linking:
- main.brs passes launch args to scene via launchArgs field
- main.brs handles roInputEvent mid-session via inputArgs field
- Supported contentId values: 'south'/'0'/'beach-camera-south' and
  'north'/'1'/'beach-camera-north'
- Works for voice search launch and cross-channel deep links

Also adds supports_input_launch=1 to manifest for Roku certification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove GNU timeout from macOS workflows

macOS runners don't have GNU coreutils timeout command.
The || true was swallowing 'command not found', causing simctl
boot to silently fail.

commit.message:
feat: bootstrap integrity verification + governance certification CLI (#99)

- IntegrityVerifier: SHA-256 hash verification of governance module
  source files and critical function bytecodes against a published
  manifest. Detects supply chain tampering at startup. (Closes #95)
- GovernanceVerifier: OWASP ASI 2026 control presence checking with
  signed attestation output, coverage percentage, and shields.io
  badge generation for README embedding
- CLI: 'agent-compliance verify' and 'agent-compliance integrity'
  commands with --json, --badge, --generate, --manifest flags
- 28 tests covering hash determinism, tamper detection, manifest
  round-trip, badge generation, and CLI integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 3 Step 2: Implement GPU command encoding tables (Gen9-Gen12)

- Created render-sys/src/cmd/ module with Intel 3D pipeline command structs
- Implemented MI commands: MI_BATCH_BUFFER_START, MI_NOOP, PIPE_CONTROL
- Implemented pipeline commands: PIPELINE_SELECT, STATE_BASE_ADDRESS
- Implemented state commands: 3DSTATE_CLIP, 3DSTATE_SF, 3DSTATE_WM, 3DSTATE_PS
- Implemented vertex commands: 3DSTATE_VERTEX_BUFFERS, 3DSTATE_VERTEX_ELEMENTS
- Implemented primitive commands: 3DPRIMITIVE with full topology support
- All commands serialize to correct binary format per Intel PRM Vol. 2
- Added 30 unit tests with 100% coverage of serialization methods
- All 60 Rust tests passing, all Go tests passing
- Zero regressions in code metrics (complexity, duplication)

References:
- Intel PRMs Volume 2 (Command Reference)
- Mesa genxml files (src/intel/genxml/*.xml)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: multi-peer gossip and force https:// for all RPC connections

- Gossip now queries ALL healthy peers (not just the active one) so a
  stale registry on one node does not hide masternodes from the wallet
- MasternodeClient::new() converts http:// -> https:// so gossip-
  discovered endpoints (previously http://) no longer fail TLS health
  checks on masternodes with rpctls enabled
- Gossip endpoints formatted as https:// directly for consistency

commit.message:
feat: channel hopping on all sensors, weighted hop pattern, improved correlation

- Both ESP32 and ESP12F hub now hop channels 1-13 with weighted pattern
  {1,2,6,3,4,11,5,6,7,11,8,9,6,10,11,12,13,1} — ch1/6/11 visited more
  often as primary non-overlapping 2.4GHz channels
- Dwell time reduced 200ms → 50ms; full cycle = 900ms
- Hub promiscuous mode toggled around wifi_set_channel() to prevent
  ESP8266 exceptions during channel changes
- yield() added in flush_remote() to feed watchdog on long flushes
- REPORT_RETRIES reduced 15 → 8 (hub cycle faster, 8 retries = ~2s
  on ch6 guaranteed to cover ≥5 hub ch6 visits)
- Hub and ESP32 REPORT_INTERVAL_MS aligned at 30s (dev) so correlator
  sees both sensors flush in same cadence
- Correlation improved: any-2 jumped from ~6% to ~20% of seen MACs,
  now covering ch 1,3,6,7,8,11,13 instead of ch6 only
- Phone-home verified: 3 clean bursts per 90s, each 2.0s wide
- Removed stray ImageMagick PostScript files (argparse/serial/sys/time)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint: clipboard, PWA, pinned messages, snippets, search, expiry

Refactor: create SQLReaper Python package from sqlmap_auto.py

Replace single-file script with full modular package:
- sqlreaper.py: CLI entry point with argparse, profiles, resume, reporting
- core/: ScanConfig dataclass, YAML profile loader, command builder, subprocess runner
- modules/: 6 attack phases (recon/injection/dump/bypass/crawl/os_access), 28 commands
- output/: logger, reporter (HTML/JSON/TXT via Jinja2 template)
- utils/: ANSI banner, input validators (URL, level, risk, threads)
- config/default.yaml: 4 scan profiles (quick/full/stealth/waf)
- tests/: 61 pytest tests (100% pass)
- requirements.txt / requirements-dev.txt / Makefile / .pre-commit-config.yaml / README.md

commit.message:
fix: move governance GET handlers before snapshot to prevent 500s

Governance API handlers (stats, config, rules, proposals) do their own
direct DB queries and don't need the admin snapshot. By hoisting them
above the getAdminSnapshot() call, any snapshot failure (DB timeout,
schema mismatch) no longer blocks governance routes.

Also removes the duplicate governance block that was further down in the
GET handler.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: auto-update homebrew-tap formula on release

Add 'update-homebrew' job that runs after a successful release:
1. Downloads checksums.txt from the new GitHub release
2. Parses sha256 for darwin/linux × amd64/arm64
3. Rewrites Formula/treemand.rb with new version + checksums
4. Commits and pushes to aallbrig/homebrew-tap via HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN

Requires HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (repo-scoped PAT with write
access to aallbrig/homebrew-tap) — already set in repo secrets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: harden workflows — security, reliability, performance

- Fix pgrep race condition: use pgrep -x for exact process name match,
  handle multiple PIDs gracefully
- Sanitize release.yml inputs: move ${{ inputs.tag_name }} to env var
  to prevent script injection (CWE-78)
- Scope permissions: top-level contents:read, per-job contents:write
  only on commit-images job (least privilege)
- Add simulator boot timeout: timeout 60 prevents CI hangs
- Simplify cache key: git tree hash instead of find+shasum (10s faster)
- Reduce schedule: weekly (Mon 6am) instead of every 12 hours

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1 complete  E2EE tunnel SFU with native client

Phase 1 (T0-T6) verified: two-way audio/video through DTLS tunnel.

SFU (str0m tunnel mode):
- ICE-only relay, forwards DTLS/SRTP/SRTCP opaquely
- SDP fingerprint + SSRC swapping for E2E DTLS
- HTTP signaling (POST /offer, GET /answer)
- Per-client packet forwarding stats

Native client (client/):
- C++ libwebrtc addon with Node.js CLI
- connect-sfu command with P2P and SFU modes
- Local preview + remote video windows (Win32 GDI)
- Two-way audio via Windows Core Audio

Project restructure:
- client/ replaces e2ee-client/ (old Insertable Streams approach)
- docs/ for architecture.md and plan.md
- Remove E2EE Planning.pdf (superseded by docs/)
- Update str0m submodule to tunnel mode commit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: gossip discovery includes inactive peers and normalises URL schemes

- Remove active=false filter: nodes restarting after an update appear
  inactive in neighbour peer lists but may already accept connections.
  The health probe discards truly unreachable nodes — no need to pre-filter.
- Normalise known-hosts dedup to ip:port (strip http/https scheme) so
  API-discovered nodes (https://) are not re-probed as gossip duplicates.
- Dedup new_endpoints before probing to prevent redundant parallel probes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 5: POC Demo App (veu-app) — integration layer

Implement VeuApp Swift Package integrating all four POC packages
(veu-crypto, veu-auth, veu-glaze, veu-ghost) into a runnable app layer.

Models:
- Identity: Ed25519 keypair generation, callsign derivation (HMAC),
  Aura seed color, device ID from SHA-256, Codable persistence
- AppState: central state managing identity, circles, Ledger, and
  circle key cache with full CRUD operations

ViewModels:
- HandshakeViewModel: full Emerald Handshake flow — initiate/respond,
  Dead Link URI generation, short code + Aura color extraction,
  confirm/reject/expire with AppState circle registration
- TimelineViewModel: compose (encrypt via Scramble + ArtifactKey),
  reload from Ledger, burn/purge, Glaze seed color derivation

Services:
- NetworkService: GhostNode lifecycle wrapper, SyncEngineDelegate
  bridge forwarding artifact/burn/sync events to app layer

Views (SwiftUI, behind #if canImport):
- HomeView: tab-based navigation (Identity, Handshake, Timeline)
- IdentityView: AuraView shader + callsign display
- HandshakeView: EmeraldView + phase-driven UI + QR/code flow
- TimelineView: Glaze grid with VueToggle reveal
- ComposeView: text input + burn timer + Seal action

22 tests passing across 3 test suites:
- IdentityTests (8): generation, derivation, Codable, signing round-trip
- HandshakeViewModelTests (6): full flow, reject, expire, reset
- TimelineViewModelTests (8): compose, burn, reload, expired purge

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Typescript repl (#20)

* upgrade 2 deps

* remove blanket ignore for markdown files

* feat: implement TypeScript REPL replacing string command dispatcher

Replace the 18 hard-coded built-in commands + JavaScript eval fallback
with a unified TypeScript evaluation pipeline:

- preload.ts: expose transpileTypeScript() via contextBridge (TypeScript
  moves from devDependencies to dependencies for runtime use in preload)
- repl-evaluator.ts: ReplEvaluator class with:
  - isComplete(): bracket/brace/paren balance with full template literal
    and comment awareness for auto-detect multi-line input
  - promoteDeclarations(): top-level const/let -> var for cross-eval
    variable persistence via inject/extract scope map
  - checkReservedNames(): errors on Bounce global name collisions
  - evaluate(): transpile -> validate -> promote -> AsyncFunction exec
    with try-return for expression value capture
- bounce-globals.d.ts: TypeScript declarations for all 19 REPL globals
- bounce-api.ts: buildBounceApi() factory wrapping all 19 typed functions
  (display, play, stop, analyze, analyzeNmf, slice, sep, nx, list,
  playSlice, playComponent, visualizeNmf, visualizeNx, onsetSlice, nmf,
  clearDebug, debug, help, clear)
- app.ts: wired in ReplEvaluator + buildBounceApi, added inputLines[]
  for multi-line accumulation with isComplete() gating, simplified
  executeCommand() to delegate to replEvaluator; removed ~2000 lines
  of string-parsing command handlers
- audio-context.ts: removed evaluate(), loadAudioFile(), analyzeOnsetSlice()
  (now owned by bounce-api.ts)
- e2e tests: updated all command strings to TypeScript function-call syntax

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix TypeScript REPL: move transpilation to main process, fix CSP, fix types

- Move TypeScript transpilation from preload require() to main process IPC
  (require('typescript') fails in Electron 40 preload context)
- Add 'unsafe-eval' to CSP to allow new AsyncFunction() in renderer
- Add triple-slash references to bounce-api.ts and audio-context.ts for
  type-checking under base tsconfig (transitively imported by test files)
- Allow triple-slash path references in ESLint (needed for ambient .d.ts)
- Update list() to also display stored features (tests expect 'nmf' in output)
- Update display() to output 'Hash:' line for test hash extraction
- transpileTypeScript signature updated to Promise<string> throughout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* make tests more deterministic

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(nav): fix sidebar scrolling on admin + tenant portals

causing nav items to be hidden/unreachable behind the footer.

and make logo + footer flex-shrink-0 (no longer absolute).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

.squad: post-task logs and merged inbox decisions

- Orchestration log: Backend DbContext lifetime fix (issue #7) — SUCCESS
- Session log: Issue #7 dbcontext-fix summary
- Merged inbox decisions (2 files) into decisions.md
- Updated architect/history.md with 2026-03-07 DbContext fix entry
- Deleted merged inbox files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deliver feature-conditional cluster events to client (#3358)

* fix: deliver feature-conditional cluster events to client

Three bugs prevented feature-conditional events (e.g. Switch initialPress,
shortRelease, multiPressComplete) from being delivered to the client even
though they were received on the wire.

## PeerBehavior: compose all events regardless of detected features

generateDiscoveredType() called ClusterComposer.compose(detectedFeatures),
so events guarded by a feature not listed in the device's featureMap were
absent from the discovered cluster type.  Unlike attributes and commands
(reported via attributeList / acceptedCommandList), there is no EventList
global attribute to discover which events a device supports.  The fix
composes with ALL feature names so every event is present, then restores
supportedFeatures to accurately reflect what the device declared.

## ClientEventEmitter: move log inside guard, warn on unknown events

The 'Received event' log was emitted before the 'if (event)' guard, so
events that had no observable (because the cluster type was incomplete)
were logged as received but silently dropped.  The log is now inside the
guard, and an explicit logger.warn is emitted for truly unknown events.

## EncodedBitmap: fix &= → |= and bigint mask off-by-one

All three bit-accumulation lines used &= instead of |=.  Because the
initial value is 0n, 0n & anything = 0n, so EncodedBitmap always returned
0 for any DecodedBitmap input.  This caused all clusters of the same type
to share one cache fingerprint in PeerBehavior.discoveredCache regardless
of their actual features.  Also fixes a bigint mask off-by-one
(2n ** BigInt(max - min - 1) → 2n ** BigInt(max - min)).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

EncodedBitmap:
- Handle single-value constraints (constraint.value) as bit positions, in
  addition to range constraints (constraint.min/max).  Real featureMap fields
  use e.g. constraint: '0' which sets constraint.value but not min/max, so
  the function was silently skipping every field and always returning 0 for
  any DecodedBitmap featureMap input.
- Fix bigint mask off-by-one: (2n**BigInt(max-min)-1n)<<BigInt(min), consistent
  with the number case 2**(max-min)-1.
- Add EncodedBitmapTest covering passthrough, single-bit flags, and the
  FeatureMap title-keyed encoding path.

PeerBehavior:
- Restore original feature composition (detected features only) and the
  deleted comment about not validating feature combinations.
- Copy events from the full-feature cluster as a separate step instead of
  composing with all features and overriding supportedFeatures — avoids
  side-effects on attributes, commands and other feature-driven logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* formatting

* Use Complete cluster events directly instead of composing all features

Composing with all feature names can fail because illegal feature
combinations may exclude events or cause compose() to throw.  Since
ClusterRegistry registers the Complete cluster variant (which already
has all events flattened with conditional markers), we can just copy
events from baseType.cluster directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(model): add multi-bit numeric and bigint coverage for EncodedBitmap (#3361)

* Initial plan

* test: add multi-bit numeric and bigint coverage for EncodedBitmap

  pointing to AppConfig.ArenaChars

  takes precedence over SAGETUI_COLOR
- docs: rename QUICK_REFERENCE.md -> ARCHITECTURE.md; fix stale header,
  correct element case count (10, not 9), expand element descriptions
- docs(README): link to ARCHITECTURE.md from Architecture section
- test: add El.lazy' value-type caching tests (int model RED->GREEN)

commit.message:
fix: use IDbContextFactory exclusively in CachedTodoService (#7)

Remove constructor-injected AppDbContext from CachedTodoService to fix
ObjectDisposedException during Blazor Server prerender-to-circuit
transitions. Each public method now creates a short-lived context via
IDbContextFactory and passes it to private methods as a parameter.

commit.message:
Compact integration-test PR comment summaries (#689)

* Initial plan

* refactor: compact PR summary for integration tests

Addresses #688 - PR comment summaries were too noisy/verbose.

Changes to both integration_tests.yml and integration_tests_docker.yml:
- Wrap performance table in <details>/<summary> collapsible section
- Group test types into categories (Multi Process, Multi Thread) as sub-headings
- Remove 'Field' column from performance table (was always 'Total execution time')
  New columns: Operation | Workers | Min (s) | Max (s) | Avg (s) | StdDev
- Show compact one-line FD test status at visible level with emoji indicator
- Wrap FD detail table in a separate collapsible section
- FD table: remove FD Before/After columns, add emoji to FD Leak column
- Aggregate performance data across all OSes/Python versions (native) or
  all distributions (docker) instead of per-distro breakdown
- Switch from python3 -c inline to proper python3 -c with YAML-valid indentation

* style: improve readability of summary Python scripts

- Split combined imports to individual import statements
- Expand single-line conditionals to multi-line for clarity
- Rename ambiguous variables 'a'/'b' to 'op_or_sess'/'sess_opt'

* compact integration-test PR summaries: collapsible sections, grouped categories, aggregated docker stats

Co-authored-by: carlkidcrypto <27721404+carlkidcrypto@users.noreply.github.com>
